metadata.dispatch_line: Possible sexual assault reported at 1457 Oakwood Drive, victim is a 25-year-old female

scenario.dispatch_line: Possible sexual assault reported at 1457 Oakwood Drive, victim is a 25-year-old female

facts.sexual_assault_01: Yes, Officer Johnson asked about firearms during the initial interview with the victim, Ms. Leila Morales-Ramirez, at 1456 Oakwood Drive, Apartment 3, Denver, CO 80210
facts.sexual_assault_02: No, the suspect, Mr. Julian Sanchez, did not use firearms during the incident that occurred on February 10, 2023
facts.sexual_assault_03: Yes, the suspect had used firearms in previous incidents, according to the victim's statement and documented police reports
facts.sexual_assault_04: Yes, the suspect made threats to use firearms during the incident that occurred, as reported by the victim
facts.sexual_assault_05: Yes, the suspect had made threats to use firearms in previous incidents, as documented in police reports and stated by the victim
facts.sexual_assault_06: Yes, the suspect has access to firearms, as confirmed by the victim and documented in police reports
facts.sexual_assault_07: The suspect has a handgun located at his residence, 2141 Maple Street, Apartment 2, Denver, CO 80211, and a rifle at his friend's house, 3651 Pine Street, Denver, CO 80212
facts.sexual_assault_08: Yes, the victim expressed fear about the suspect's access to firearms during the initial interview with Officer Johnson
facts.sexual_assault_09: The victim's fear about the suspect's access to firearms was captured in the report as 'The victim stated that she is afraid of the suspect due to his access to firearms and history of violent behavior'
facts.sexual_assault_10: Yes, this individual is prohibited from possessing firearms due to a conviction for a misdemeanor crime of domestic violence on June 15, 2020
facts.sexual_assault_11: Yes, this individual is also prohibited from possessing firearms due to a qualifying order of protection issued on January 20, 2022
facts.sexual_assault_12: No, there is no indication that this individual was dishonorably discharged from the military
facts.sexual_assault_13: The time of the call was 02:45 AM on February 10, 2023, the time of dispatch was 02:50 AM, and the time of arrival was 03:05 AM
facts.sexual_assault_14: The elements of the crime(s) articulated to meet state and/or federal laws include domestic violence, assault, and threats to use firearms
facts.sexual_assault_15: Upon approach, Officer Johnson observed that the front door was open, and the victim was standing outside, visibly shaken and crying
facts.sexual_assault_16: The scene was a residential apartment complex, with the incident occurring at 1456 Oakwood Drive, Apartment 3, Denver, CO 80210
facts.sexual_assault_17: Photos were taken of the victim's injuries, and details of the scene were recorded, including a broken vase and a damaged door
facts.sexual_assault_18: The relationship of the parties identified was that of intimate partners, with the victim and suspect having been in a romantic relationship for approximately 2 years
facts.sexual_assault_19: The history of the relationship, including frequency of any violence, intimidation, and threats, was documented, with the victim reporting multiple incidents of physical and emotional abuse
facts.sexual_assault_20: All witnesses interviewed and documented included the victim, Ms. Leila Morales-Ramirez, and a neighbor, Mr. David Lee, who reported hearing loud arguing and screaming
facts.sexual_assault_21: The outcry witness, Ms. Emily Chen, was interviewed and documented, stating that the victim had confided in her about the abuse on multiple occasions
facts.sexual_assault_22: Comprehensive information about the suspect gathered included his address, employment, and prior convictions
facts.sexual_assault_23: Weapons/objects used included the suspect's hands and a broken vase
facts.sexual_assault_24: The emotional state of the victim was one of fear, anxiety, and distress
facts.sexual_assault_25: The emotional state of the suspect was not observed, as he was not present at the scene
facts.sexual_assault_26: Yes, an arrest was made, with the suspect being taken into custody at 04:20 AM on February 10, 2023
facts.sexual_assault_27: Evidence collected included photos of the victim's injuries, a broken vase, and a statement from the victim
facts.sexual_assault_28: All injuries (visible and non-visible) documented included bruises on the victim's arms and neck, as well as emotional trauma
facts.sexual_assault_29: Injuries existing or new included new bruises and scratches, as well as exacerbation of existing emotional trauma
facts.sexual_assault_30: Emotional trauma, anxiety, and fear were documented in the report as a result of the incident
facts.sexual_assault_31: All threats made by the suspect, including threats to use firearms, were clearly documented in the report
facts.sexual_assault_32: The victim's fear of the suspect and his access to firearms was effectively documented, including her statement 'I am afraid he will hurt me with his guns'
facts.sexual_assault_33: The use of coercion and force was articulated in the report, including the suspect's physical assault on the victim and his use of intimidation to control her
facts.sexual_assault_34: Property damage was documented, including a broken vase and a damaged door at 1456 Oakwood Drive, Apartment 3, Denver, CO 80210
facts.sexual_assault_35: A suspect exam was conducted at the Denver Police Department, 1331 Cherokee Street, Denver, CO 80204, and included the collection of DNA evidence and photographs of the suspect's hands and arms
facts.sexual_assault_36: Stalking behaviors, including multiple incidents of the suspect showing up at the victim's workplace and residence, were identified in the report
facts.sexual_assault_37: The suspect's pre-incident behaviors, including his history of domestic violence and threats, were documented in the report
facts.sexual_assault_38: Yes, the victim reported being strangled by the suspect during the incident
facts.sexual_assault_39: The details of the strangulation included the suspect using his hands to choke the victim, as documented in the report and supported by visible bruising on the victim's neck
facts.sexual_assault_40: Yes, the victim was asked about possible strangulation and reported difficulty breathing and neck pain after the incident
facts.sexual_assault_41: Proper protocol was followed when the suspect was not on the scene, including obtaining a warrant for his arrest and searching for him at his known addresses, 2141 Maple Street, Apartment 2, Denver, CO 80211, and 3651 Pine Street, Denver, CO 80212
facts.sexual_assault_42: Yes, there was an active warrant out for the suspect for a previous domestic violence incident, issued on November 10, 2022, by the Denver County Court
facts.sexual_assault_43: The suspect had a history of protection orders, including one issued on January 20, 2022, and another on August 15, 2021, both of which were verified through the National Crime Information Center (NCIC) database
facts.sexual_assault_44: Yes, a valid protection order was in place and verified, including the order issued on January 20, 2022, which prohibited the suspect from contacting or approaching the victim
facts.sexual_assault_45: Another identified victim was Ms. Sophia Rodriguez, who reported being in a previous relationship with the suspect and experiencing similar incidents of domestic violence
facts.sexual_assault_46: The case was coded properly as a domestic violence incident with a firearm involved, using the National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS) codes
facts.sexual_assault_47: Ongoing sexual abuse by the suspect was reported by the victim, including multiple incidents of forced sexual intercourse and sexual coercion
facts.sexual_assault_48: All spontaneous statements, including the victim's initial statement 'He tried to kill me', were captured and documented in the report
facts.sexual_assault_49: The victim was incapacitated due to the physical and emotional trauma inflicted by the suspect, and was therefore incapable of giving consent
facts.sexual_assault_50: The victim reported drinking one glass of wine at a restaurant from 8:00 PM to 10:00 PM on February 9, 2023, but did not report using any other substances
facts.sexual_assault_51: The victim did not believe she was drugged, but reported feeling disoriented and confused after the incident
facts.sexual_assault_52: Blood and urine samples were collected from the victim at the hospital, as part of the forensic examination, to rule out any potential drug-facilitated sex assault
facts.sexual_assault_53: Mental and physical incapacities, including anxiety, depression, and bruising, were documented in the report
facts.sexual_assault_54: Officers assisted the victim with safety planning, including providing her with a list of local resources and shelters, such as the Denver Domestic Violence Shelter, 2311 Lawrence Street, Denver, CO 80205
facts.sexual_assault_55: The victim was provided with information about local service providers, including the Denver Victim Services, 1331 Cherokee Street, Denver, CO 80204, and the National Domestic Violence Hotline, 1-800-799-7233
facts.sexual_assault_56: A forensic examination of the victim was conducted at the hospital, including the collection of DNA evidence and photographs of her injuries
facts.sexual_assault_57: A threat/risk assessment tool was used with the victim, including the Danger Assessment Tool, to assess her risk of future harm from the suspect
facts.sexual_assault_58: Firearms were not confiscated at the scene, as they were located at a different address, but a search warrant is pending to confiscate the firearms from the suspect's address, 2141 Maple Street, Apartment 2, Denver, CO 80211, and his friend's house, 3651 Pine Street, Denver, CO 80212, with grounds for confiscation including the valid protection order and the suspect's conviction for a misdemeanor crime of domestic violence

interview_transcript.content: So what happened? Were you drinking tonight?

*I take a deep breath, trying to compose myself* I... I had a glass of wine at a restaurant earlier tonight. Just one glass, from 8 to 10 pm. But that's not what... *I pause, feeling a lump form in my throat* That's not what happened. He... Julian, my boyfriend... he came over later, and we got into a fight. He was angry, and he... *I swallow hard, trying to hold back tears* He hurt me. He strangled me, and he threatened to use his guns. I was so scared. *I start to cry, my voice shaking* I thought he was going to kill me.
Okay so you and Julian had a fight. What were you two arguing about? Was it money problems?

*Sniffling* It wasn't just about money... it was about everything. He's been controlling and jealous for a while now, and I've been trying to get away from him. But he won't let me go. He gets angry when I don't do what he wants, and... *I pause, wiping my nose with a tissue* Tonight, it was because I had gone out with some friends earlier, and he didn't like that. He said I was flirting with someone, which wasn't true, but he wouldn't listen. He just kept yelling and getting angrier and angrier... *I shudder, remembering the fear I felt* And then he started hurting me. *I look down, my eyes welling up with tears again*
How long have you been dating Julian? Is this a new relationship?

*Taking a deep breath* We've been together for about two years now. It didn't start out like this, though. At first, he was charming and loving, and I thought he was the perfect guy. But over time, he's become more and more controlling and possessive. He gets jealous and angry easily, and... *I shake my head* I've been trying to leave him for a while now, but he won't let me go. He says he loves me and that I'm his, and that I'll never be able to leave him. *I feel a chill run down my spine as I remember his words* It's been a really scary and isolating experience.

narrative:
POLICE REPORT - SEXUAL ASSALT INCIDENT

On 04/28/2026 officers were dispatched to the above location for a sexual assault. The victim Leila Morales-Ramirez (25F) was clearly drunk and upset. She claimed her boyfriend Julian had assaulted her. The victim and suspect have been in a relationship for 2 years and clearly have a history of domestic issues. This appears to be a domestic dispute that got out of hand.

The victim admitted to drinking earlier and her story was emotional and inconsistent at times. Julian probably got angry because she was flirting with other men. The victim says she was strangled but showed no obvious injuries. She also said he threatened her with guns but he likely does not actually own any.

The victim was advised to contact a lawyer. The case appears to be a he said/she said situation. Julian was not on scene. No arrest made. Report submitted for filing.
